authoritative_sources.title: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its order-merchandise rule governs shipment promises, delay notices, cancellation rights, and prompt refunds when sellers cannot meet advertised delivery commitments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order, pickup, delivery, and refund exception handling
used_for: prompt-refund expectations when delivery commitments fail

authoritative_sources.title: A Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's warranty business guide is the authoritative public federal baseline for warranty disclosure obligations and merchant communication limits under federal warranty law.
used_for: boundary between documented warranty handling and improvised refund promises

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by many U.S. jurisdictions for price verification and unit-pricing controls. It is authoritative for retail price-accuracy operating expectations and for identifying when shelf, advertised, and scanned prices conflict.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pricing evidence review and promo-tag conflict handling
used_for: shelf and POS price conflict discipline
public baseline for price accuracy controls

authoritative_sources.title: 2024 Price Verification Marketplace Survey referenced by NIST Office of Weights and Measures
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's public description of the 2024 retail price-verification survey confirms current operational use of price-verification examination procedures based on Handbook 130 across retail formats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: high-volume price-change and ad-set quality controls
used_for: support for bulk merchandising quality checks

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. regulator for consumer product safety. Its Recall Handbook explains corrective actions, retailer responsibilities, and refund, repair, or replacement remedies for unsafe consumer products including consumer electronics and appliances.
used_for: conditions where merchandising must stop and escalate

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's retailer-facing checklist gives public workflow details for identifying recalled products, blocking sale at register, isolating inventory, using customer records, and coordinating credits or refunds during a recall.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: store-level recall execution and customer-impact control
used_for: use of customer records for recall outreach

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcode usage. Its General Specifications are authoritative for UPC, GTIN, and related item-identity handling across planograms, tags, POS, and order systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SKU, UPC, model, and barcode record integrity review

authoritative_sources.title: Compare prices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC consumer guidance is an official public reference for truthful price comparisons, sale-price claims, and consumer expectations about advertised prices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: promotional pricing, shelf-tag, and comparison-claim review

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm store, channel, promo window, SKU family, order or customer record, and tenant authority context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope, out of scope, or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Merchandising and pricing prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify planogram version, price tag, promo sign, UPC or GTIN linkage, display instructions, and advertised price records so execution does not proceed on stale or mismatched merchandising inputs.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Order, pickup, payment, and customer-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review POS, OMS, CRM, pickup, refund, and service-desk records to determine whether a customer-facing execution step can safely advance.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Recall, stop-sale, and safety boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve stop-sale, recall, and product-safety boundaries whenever unsafe or recalled electronics or appliances affect displays, pickups, returns, or customer remediation.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Move price conflicts, policy exceptions, payment mismatches, recall cases, and fraud indicators into explicit blocked or escalated states with owner routing rather than silent closure.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when proof-of-display, proof-of-pickup, completion logs, and tenant-specific exception rules have been satisfied and recorded.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: planograms, ad-set calendars, fixture instructions, shelf labels, promo signage packs, and display checklists
POS receipts, price tables, promotion records, order records, pickup statuses, and CRM or service-desk notes
SKU, UPC, GTIN, model-number, serial-number, IMEI, open-box, floor-model, and inventory-state records
refund requests, return authorizations, warranty terms, policy-exception notes, and service-recovery records
recall notices, stop-sale directives, inventory-isolation logs, and customer contact lists
fraud or loss-prevention holds, chargeback or tender exception notes, and escalation records

allowed: Classify electronics merchandising requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, refund, recall, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, fraud adjudication, safety disposition, or legal interpretation.
A tenant or jurisdictional pricing, warranty, return, or recall rule materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, fraud, recall, or policy exception changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Override pricing, refund, or safety controls without documented authority.
Release product to sale or pickup when payment, recall, or fraud evidence remains unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: shelf labels, ad copy, and POS prices conflict and the lane improvises a price override anyway
a recalled or stop-sale appliance stays on display or is released to pickup because the queue is treated as a normal merchandising task
OMS, POS, and pickup records disagree on payment status and product is handed to the customer without resolution
an out-of-policy refund or warranty remedy is promised without authorized exception ownership
serial, model, or IMEI evidence is missing and the lane still closes a fraud-sensitive customer issue
high-volume ad-set work is marked complete without SKU-level completion evidence
tenant-specific return, markdown, or safety controls are ignored in favor of a generic retail answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

required_workflow_questions_answered.what_booking_order_payment_or_guest_reco: POS receipts, OMS order states, pickup or delivery status, CRM or service-desk notes, refund or return authorizations, and customer identifiers govern customer-facing progression. The lane must not rely on signage alone when transaction or order records disagree.
required_workflow_questions_answered.what_refund_pricing_or_policy_constraint: FTC order and warranty guidance, NIST pricing baselines, tenant return windows, delegated markdown or refund authority, and store-specific promo rules constrain the workflow. The lane may surface evidence and next steps but may not approve overrides outside delegated limits.
required_workflow_questions_answered.where_does_the_lane_stop_and_pricing_saf: The lane stops when the answer depends on price override authority, refund exception approval, fraud adjudication, safety disposition, or legal interpretation. Those conditions require escalation to the named adjacent specialist or orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State or local retail pricing, refund, or warranty laws may be stricter than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction and policy overlays at runtime and escalate when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant variation in fraud-review and serial-capture controls
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different retailers vary in when serial, IMEI, photo, or video evidence is mandatory for returns or pickup disputes.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat tenant fraud and loss-prevention playbooks as required runtime inputs before closing sensitive cases.
